steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes a key contrast: the building's impracticality (designed to be “more fanciful than functional”) produces tangible health benefits, as shown by Tanaka's “significant health benefits” after four years.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: Although impractical, the design of the apartment building by Mora and Arai may improve the well-being of the building's residents. directly expresses this central claim—an impractical design can improve well-being. The other three options either introduce unsupported claims or mischaracterize the evidence. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
